revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「 '''鎌倉型横穴墓'''（かまくらがたよこあなぼ）とは、[[鎌倉郡]]であった[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]]とその周辺部に存在…」
revision.text:
'''鎌倉型横穴墓'''（かまくらがたよこあなぼ）とは、[[鎌倉郡]]であった[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]]とその周辺部に存在する、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]始め頃に造られた[[横穴墓]]の一種で「棺室」があるのを特徴とする。「鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓」とも。
==デザイン== 神奈川県では、古墳時代から奈良時代始めに山の斜面に洞窟型墓地である『横穴墓群』が多く造られた。このなかの「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるデザインは、[[羨道]]（入口）から入った死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と、[[蒲鉾]]形の天井をもち、さらに奥の壁には、「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小さな部屋を掘り造っているのを特徴とするものである。
ちなみに鎌倉には、[[鎌倉時代]]に[[やぐら]]と呼ばれる直方体洞窟型の墓地も造られるが、横穴墓とやぐらの間に系統的な関係はない<ref>栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20～21ページ</ref>。
==呼び方の変遷== 昔は神奈川県東南部を流れる[[㹨川]]（[[横浜市]][[栄区]]）の流域に見つかることが多く、この付近の地名[[鍛冶ケ谷]]をとって「鍛冶ヶ谷式」と呼ばれたが、2004年（平成16年）ごろの研究で、鎌倉市を中心とした[[鎌倉郡]]を中心に広がっていることがわかったため「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるようになってきた<ref>栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20～21ページ</ref><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20～30ページ</ref>。
==鎌倉型横穴墓の例== *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] **[[七石山横穴墓群]]など<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1～3ページ</ref>
==詳しく載っている本== *[[鵠沼女子高等学校]]地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 *神奈川県考古学会『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考資料== *栄の歴史編集委員会『栄の歴史』栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『栄区の重要遺跡』2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行
{{デフォルトソート:かまくらかたよこあなほ}}

==参考資料== *栄の歴史編集委員会『栄の歴史』栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『栄区の重要遺跡』2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行
{{デフォルトソート:かまくらかたよこあなほ}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:神奈川県の古墳]]

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]の鎌倉型。手前の[[羨道]]と[[玄室]]のほとんどが崩壊しているので奥の「棺室」が見えやすい。]]
'''鎌倉型横穴墓'''（かまくらがたよこあなぼ）とは、[[鎌倉郡]]であった[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]]とその周辺部に存在する、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]始めごろに造られた[[横穴墓]]の一種で、[[玄室]]の奥壁に「棺室」を持つのを特徴とする。「鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓」とも。
==デザイン== 神奈川県では、古墳時代から奈良時代始めに山の斜面に洞窟型墓地である『横穴墓群』が多く造られた。このなかの「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるデザインは、[[羨道]]（入口）から入った死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と、[[蒲鉾]]形の天井をもち、さらに奥の壁には、「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小さな部屋を掘り造っているのを特徴とするものである。
ちなみに鎌倉には、[[鎌倉時代]]に[[やぐら]]と呼ばれる直方体洞窟型の墓地も造られるが、横穴墓とやぐらの間に系統的な関係はない<ref>栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>。
==呼び方の変遷== 昔は神奈川県東南部を流れる[[㹨川]]（[[横浜市]][[栄区]]）の流域に見つかることが多く、この付近の地名[[鍛冶ケ谷]]をとって「鍛冶ヶ谷式」と呼ばれたが、2004年（平成16年）ごろの研究で、鎌倉市を中心とした[[鎌倉郡]]を中心に広がっていることがわかったため「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるようになってきた<ref>栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>。
==鎌倉型横穴墓の例== *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] **[[七石山横穴墓群]]・[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]など<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==詳しく載っている本== *[[鵠沼女子高等学校]]地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 *神奈川県考古学会『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考資料== *栄の歴史編集委員会『栄の歴史』栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『栄区の重要遺跡』2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行
{{デフォルトソート:かまくらかたよこあなほ}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:神奈川県の古墳]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]]

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]の鎌倉型。手前の[[羨道]]と[[玄室]]のほとんどが崩壊しているので奥の「棺室」が見えやすい。]]
'''鎌倉型横穴墓'''（かまくらがたよこあなぼ）とは、[[鎌倉郡]]であった[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]]とその周辺部に存在する、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]始めごろに造られた[[横穴墓]]の一種で、[[玄室]]の奥壁に「棺室」を持つのを特徴とする。「鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓」とも。
==デザイン== 神奈川県では、古墳時代から奈良時代始めに山の斜面に洞窟型墓地である『横穴墓群』が多く造られた。このなかの「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるデザインは、[[羨道]]（入口）から入った死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と、[[蒲鉾]]形の天井をもち、さらに奥の壁には、「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小さな部屋を掘り造っているのを特徴とするものである。
ちなみに鎌倉には、[[鎌倉時代]]に[[やぐら]]と呼ばれる直方体洞窟型の墓地も造られるが、横穴墓とやぐらの間に系統的な関係はない<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>。
==呼び方の変遷== 昔は神奈川県東南部を流れる[[㹨川]]（[[横浜市]][[栄区]]）の流域に見つかることが多く、この付近の地名[[鍛冶ケ谷]]をとって「鍛冶ヶ谷式」と呼ばれたが、2004年（平成16年）ごろの研究で、鎌倉市を中心とした[[鎌倉郡]]を中心に広がっていることがわかったため「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるようになってきた<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>。
==鎌倉型横穴墓の例== *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] **[[七石山横穴墓群]]・[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]など<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==詳しく載っている本== *[[鵠沼女子高等学校]]地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 *神奈川県考古学会『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行

==参考資料== *栄の歴史編集委員会『栄の歴史』栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『栄区の重要遺跡』2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}} '''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈..."
revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}
{{Japanese Cultural Properties}} {{coord|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist]]

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist]]

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist]]
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist]]
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:港南台遺跡群]] * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist]]
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).

== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]]tive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq-Kamakura-style cave tombs-qq]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}の鎌倉型。手前の{{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}}と{{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}のほとんどが崩壊しているので奥の「棺室」が見えやすい。]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}の鎌倉型。手前の{{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}}と{{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}のほとんどが崩壊しているので奥の「棺室」が見えやすい。
Kamakura type of Miyanomae side hole tombs. Most of the entrance and entrance chambers in the foreground have collapsed, so it is easy to see the "coffin room" in the back.
]]
