[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster" to "Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster"
revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行

They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Tunnel Tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16年）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓)

=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
