[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=draft}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾|12=draft}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら|12=draft}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=draft}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|12=draft}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=draft}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=draft}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=draft}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾|12=draft|12=draft}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら|12=draft|12=draft}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|12=draft|12=draft}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=draft|12=draft}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=draft|12=draft}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=draft|12=draft}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓)
*

==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|12=draft|12=draft}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=draft|12=draft}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾|12=draft|12=draft}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら|12=draft|12=draft}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|12=draft|12=draft}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=draft|12=draft}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

{{Kofun navbar]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tunnel Tombs]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Ancient Tombs of Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Archaeological Sites in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kōnan Ward, Yokohama]] [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Cave tomb clusters]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster forks]] [[Category:Kamakura-style cave tombs]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Kamakura-style cave tombs]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]] [[ja:玄室]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]] [[ja:横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]] [[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] [[ja:港南区]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] [[ja:栄区]] [[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] [[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] [[ja:函南町]] [[ja:北江間横穴群]] [[ja:伊豆の国市]] [[ja:江浦横穴群]] [[ja:沼津市]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] [[ja:加賀市]] [[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏原市]] [[ja:安福寺横穴群]] [[ja:十王免横穴群]] [[ja:松江市]] [[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] [[ja:出雲市]] [[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] [[ja:知夫村]] [[ja:竹並遺跡]] [[ja:行橋市]] [[ja:城山横穴群]] [[ja:福智町]] [[ja:水町遺跡]] [[ja:直方市]] [[ja:古月横穴]] [[ja:鞍手町]] [[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] [[ja:玉名市]] [[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] [[ja:鍋田横穴群]] [[ja:山鹿市]] [[ja:大村横穴群]] [[ja:人吉市]] [[ja:四日市横穴群]] [[ja:宇佐市]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[en:Kamakura-style cave tombs]]

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible.
]]
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾|12=draft|12=draft}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら|12=draft|12=draft}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|12=draft|12=draft}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=draft|12=draft}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=draft|12=draft}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=draft|12=draft}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾|12=draft|12=draft}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら|12=draft|12=draft}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|12=draft|12=draft}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=draft|12=draft}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

{{Kofun navbar]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tunnel Tombs]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Ancient Tombs of Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Archaeological Sites in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kōnan Ward, Yokohama]] [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Cave tomb clusters]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster forks]] [[Category:Kamakura-style cave tombs]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamakura-style cave tombs]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]] [[ja:玄室]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]] [[ja:横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]] [[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] [[ja:港南区]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] [[ja:栄区]] [[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] [[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] [[ja:函南町]] [[ja:北江間横穴群]] [[ja:伊豆の国市]] [[ja:江浦横穴群]] [[ja:沼津市]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] [[ja:加賀市]] [[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏原市]] [[ja:安福寺横穴群]] [[ja:十王免横穴群]] [[ja:松江市]] [[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] [[ja:出雲市]] [[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] [[ja:知夫村]] [[ja:竹並遺跡]] [[ja:行橋市]] [[ja:城山横穴群]] [[ja:福智町]] [[ja:水町遺跡]] [[ja:直方市]] [[ja:古月横穴]] [[ja:鞍手町]] [[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] [[ja:玉名市]] [[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] [[ja:鍋田横穴群]] [[ja:山鹿市]] [[ja:大村横穴群]] [[ja:人吉市]] [[ja:四日市横穴群]] [[ja:宇佐市]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[en:Kamakura-style cave tombs]]

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=draft|12=draft}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=draft|12=draft}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾|12=draft|12=draft}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら|12=draft|12=draft}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|12=draft|12=draft}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=draft|12=draft}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=draft|12=draft}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行

They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=draft|12=draft}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=draft|12=draft}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=draft|12=draft}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) *
== See Also == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en|12=draft|12=draft}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> ) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=draft|12=draft}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> * {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|en|12=draft|12=draft}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs-->

== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en|12=draft|12=draft}} <!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]の鎌倉型。手前の[[羨道]]と[[玄室]]のほとんどが崩壊しているので奥の「棺室」が見えやすい。]]
'''鎌倉型横穴墓'''（かまくらがたよこあなぼ）とは、[[鎌倉郡]]であった[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]]とその周辺部に存在する、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]始めごろに造られた[[横穴墓]]の一種で、[[玄室]]の奥壁に「棺室」を持つのを特徴とする。「鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓」とも。
==デザイン== 神奈川県では、古墳時代から奈良時代始めに山の斜面に洞窟型墓地である『横穴墓群』が多く造られた。このなかの「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるデザインは、[[羨道]]（入口）から入った死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と、[[蒲鉾]]形の天井をもち、さらに奥の壁には、「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小さな部屋を掘り造っているのを特徴とするものである。
ちなみに鎌倉には、[[鎌倉時代]]に[[やぐら]]と呼ばれる直方体洞窟型の墓地も造られるが、横穴墓とやぐらの間に系統的な関係はない<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>。
==呼び方の変遷== 昔は神奈川県東南部を流れる[[㹨川]]（[[横浜市]][[栄区]]）の流域に見つかることが多く、この付近の地名[[鍛冶ケ谷]]をとって「鍛冶ヶ谷式」と呼ばれたが、2004年（平成16年）ごろの研究で、鎌倉市を中心とした[[鎌倉郡]]を中心に広がっていることがわかったため「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるようになってきた<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015年）20-30ページ</ref>。
==鎌倉型横穴墓の例== *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] **[[七石山横穴墓群]]・[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]など<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==詳しく載っている本== *[[鵠沼女子高等学校]]地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 *神奈川県考古学会『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考資料== *栄の歴史編集委員会『栄の歴史』栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『栄区の重要遺跡』2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行
{{デフォルトソート:かまくらかたよこあなほ}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:神奈川県の古墳]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]]

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]の鎌倉型。手前の[[羨道]]と[[玄室]]のほとんどが崩壊しているので奥の「棺室」が見えやすい。]]
'''鎌倉型横穴墓'''（かまくらがたよこあなぼ）とは、[[鎌倉郡]]であった[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]]とその周辺部に存在する、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]始めごろに造られた[[横穴墓]]の一種で、[[玄室]]の奥壁に「棺室」を持つのを特徴とする。「鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓」とも。
==デザイン== 神奈川県では、古墳時代から奈良時代始めに山の斜面に洞窟型墓地である『横穴墓群』が多く造られた。このなかの「鎌倉型」と呼ばれるデザインは、[[羨道]]（入口）から入った死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と、[[蒲鉾]]形の天井をもち、さらに奥の壁には、「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小さな部屋を掘り造っているのを特徴とするものである。
ちなみに鎌倉には、[[鎌倉時代]]に[[やぐら]]と呼ばれる直方体洞窟型の墓地も造られるが、横穴墓とやぐらの間に系統的な関係はない<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20-21ページ</ref>。

==鎌倉型横穴墓の例== *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] **[[七石山横穴墓群]]・[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]など<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>
==詳しく載っている本== *[[鵠沼女子高等学校]]地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 *神奈川県考古学会『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004年（平成16年）4月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考資料== *栄の歴史編集委員会『栄の歴史』栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『栄区の重要遺跡』2015年（平成27年）11月13日発行

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Period|qq=}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Period|qq=}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura City|qq=}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamaboko|qq=}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Period|qq=}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagura|qq=}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City|qq=}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Ward|qq=}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Period|qq=}} to the early {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Period|qq=}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura City|qq=}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|ja|蒲鉾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamaboko|qq=}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Period|qq=}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura|ja|やぐら|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagura|qq=}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City|qq=}} and {{ill|Sakae Ward|ja|栄区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Ward|qq=}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|qq=}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行

[[sn:Kamakura-style cave tombs]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]] [[ja:玄室]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]] [[ja:横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]] [[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] [[ja:港南区]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] [[ja:栄区]] [[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] [[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] [[ja:函南町]] [[ja:北江間横穴群]] [[ja:伊豆の国市]] [[ja:江浦横穴群]] [[ja:沼津市]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] [[ja:加賀市]] [[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏原市]] [[ja:安福寺横穴群]] [[ja:十王免横穴群]] [[ja:松江市]] [[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] [[ja:出雲市]] [[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] [[ja:知夫村]] [[ja:竹並遺跡]] [[ja:行橋市]] [[ja:城山横穴群]] [[ja:福智町]] [[ja:水町遺跡]] [[ja:直方市]] [[ja:古月横穴]] [[ja:鞍手町]] [[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] [[ja:玉名市]] [[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] [[ja:鍋田横穴群]] [[ja:山鹿市]] [[ja:大村横穴群]] [[ja:人吉市]] [[ja:四日市横穴群]] [[ja:宇佐市]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[en:Kamakura-style cave tombs]]
<!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs-->

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|qq=}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|qq=}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) *

{{Japanese Cultural Properties]] {{coord|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E|region:JP-14|display=title]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiya Cave Tombs|qq=}}
({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * [[:en:Hōōzan Cave Tombs|Hōōzan Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|qq=}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|qq=}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行

revision.text:
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|qq=}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行

[[sn:Kamakura-style cave tombs]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]] [[ja:玄室]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]] [[ja:横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]] [[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] [[ja:港南区]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] [[ja:栄区]] [[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] [[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] [[ja:函南町]] [[ja:北江間横穴群]] [[ja:伊豆の国市]] [[ja:江浦横穴群]] [[ja:沼津市]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] [[ja:加賀市]] [[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏原市]] [[ja:安福寺横穴群]] [[ja:十王免横穴群]] [[ja:松江市]] [[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] [[ja:出雲市]] [[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] [[ja:知夫村]] [[ja:竹並遺跡]] [[ja:行橋市]] [[ja:城山横穴群]] [[ja:福智町]] [[ja:水町遺跡]] [[ja:直方市]] [[ja:古月横穴]] [[ja:鞍手町]] [[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] [[ja:玉名市]] [[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] [[ja:鍋田横穴群]] [[ja:山鹿市]] [[ja:大村横穴群]] [[ja:人吉市]] [[ja:四日市横穴群]] [[ja:宇佐市]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[en:Kamakura-style cave tombs]]
<!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--><!--Kamakura-style cave tombs--> [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Kanagawa prefecture]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Kanagawa prefecture]]

[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|qq=}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|qq=}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|qq=}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|qq=}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|qq=}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
