<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) *

== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiya Cave Tombs|qq=}}
({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * [[:en:Hōōzan Cave Tombs|Hōōzan Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”

In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行
'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) *

== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiya Cave Tombs|qq=}}
({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * [[:en:Hōōzan Cave Tombs|Hōōzan Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]])

Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site

It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>

'''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q97576952}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kanagawa prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97576952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara Period]], found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]], and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a [[:en:Kamaboko|Kamaboko]]. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a [[:en:Yagura (tombs)|Yagura]] was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] and [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae Ward]]. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行
==Footnotes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * 栄の歴史編集委員会 『栄の歴史』 栄区地域振興課 2013rd year （平成25th year ）3月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}} 『栄区の重要遺跡』 2015th year （平成27th year ）11月13日発行

== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun Period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|en|Kamaboko|WD=Q166204|lt=Kamaboko}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura (tombs)|en|Yagura (tombs)|WD=Q3571304|lt=Yagura}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}} and {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae Ward}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
==Books with Detailed Information== * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} 地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964th year （昭和39th year ）3月20日発行 * 神奈川県考古学会 『考古論叢神奈河 12集』（田村良照「相模の横穴墓」）2004th year （平成16th year ）4月発行 * {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|lt=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|lt_ja=埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}} 『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』 2015th year （平成27th year ）3月20日発行

The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|en|Kamaboko|WD=Q166204|lt=Kamaboko}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura (tombs)|en|Yagura (tombs)|WD=Q3571304|lt=Yagura}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}} and {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae Ward}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]]
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun Period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|en|Kamaboko|WD=Q166204|lt=Kamaboko}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura (tombs)|en|Yagura (tombs)|WD=Q3571304|lt=Yagura}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}} and {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae Ward}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]]
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun Period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|en|Kamaboko|WD=Q166204|lt=Kamaboko}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura (tombs)|en|Yagura (tombs)|WD=Q3571304|lt=Yagura}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}} and {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae Ward}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]]
[[File:宮ノ前横穴墓群-2.jpg|thumb|250px|Kamakura-style of the {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}]]. Since most of the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} and {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}} at the front have collapsed, the inner "coffin chamber" is easily visible. '''Kamakura-style Tunnel Tomb''' (鎌倉型横穴墓) refers to a type of {{ill|Cave tomb|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} constructed from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun Period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period}}, found in the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}—present-day {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City}}, and their surrounding areas. It is characterized by having a “coffin chamber” excavated into the rear wall of the {{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}, and is also known as the “Kaji-ga-tani-style Tunnel Tomb.”
==Design== In Kanagawa Prefecture, many cave-type cemeteries—referred to as “Tunnel Tomb Clusters”—were constructed on mountainsides from the Kofun Period to the early Nara Period. The design known as the “Kamakura-style” is characterized by a mortuary passage (入口) leading from the {{ill|Senshido|ja|羨道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshido|1=endō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609923}} into the burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshitsu|qq=}}), which features an elongated trapezoidal floor and a ceiling in the shape of a {{ill|Kamaboko|en|Kamaboko|WD=Q166204|lt=Kamaboko}}. In addition, a small room resembling a shelf, known as the “coffin chamber” (棺室), is excavated into the back wall.
Incidentally, during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, a rectangular cave-type cemetery known as a {{ill|Yagura (tombs)|en|Yagura (tombs)|WD=Q3571304|lt=Yagura}} was also built in Kamakura; however, there is no systematic relationship between the tunnel tombs and the yagura.<ref name="#1">栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013rd year ）20-21ページ</ref>
==Evolution of the Name== In the past, these tombs were frequently discovered in the basin of the {{ill|Kassakawa|ja|㹨川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa|1=Itachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11259475}}, which flows through southeastern Kanagawa Prefecture, particularly in the area of {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}} and {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae Ward}}. They were named after the local area {{ill|Kaji-ga-tani|ja|鍛冶ケ谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaji-ga-tani|1=Kajigaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650738}}, being called “Kaji-ga-tani-style.” However, research conducted around 2004 (Heisei 16) revealed that these tombs are widespread mainly in the area centered on Kamakura City within the former {{ill|Kamakura District|ja|鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District|1=Kamakura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175662}}, leading to the adoption of the name “Kamakura-style.”<ref name="#1"/><ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『栄区の重要遺跡』（2015th year ）20-30ページ</ref>
==Examples of Kamakura-style Tunnel Tombs== * {{ill|Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kassakawa Basin Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576871}} ** {{ill|Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanasekiyama Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353656}} ・ {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}}<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015th year ）1-3ページ</ref>
